title: J. ANDRADE-SÁNCHEZ”
text: My name is Jorge Andrade Sánchez, a wildlife ecologist from Baja California. My father’s family (Andrade-Murillo) is one of the oldest families from Baja California Sur, particularly from La Paz and Santa Rosalía (Choyero lands). My mother’s side, the Sánchez-Servín family, took a long journey from Nahuatzen, Michoacan to stablish at San Vicentito, Baja California to latter migrate (in burro) to El Rosario, Baja California back in 1945. I grew up in Ensenada but travelling coastal roads across the peninsula visiting my family. The wonders of the peninsula, including its wildlife, enviroments and its remarkable people, have had a profound impact on me and made me the person and researcher I am now. In this personal webpage I’ll share about my research, photography, family history and opinions.

title: About
text: Hello y’all! My name is Jorge Andrade Sánchez. My father’s family (Andrade-Murillo) came from one of the oldest families from Baja California Sur (the Murillo family), particularly from La Paz and Santa Rosalía (Choyero lands). My mother’s side, the Sánchez-Servín family, took a long journey from Nahuatzen, Michoacan to stablish in San Vicentito, Baja California and latter in El Rosario, Baja California in 1945. I grew up in Ensenada but travelling coastal roads across the peninsula visiting my family. In a sense, I am a true “Bajacaliforniano”. The wonders of the peninsula, including the wildlife, enviroments and its remarkable people have had a profound impact on me and made me how I am. In this, personal webpage, I’ll share about my research, photography, opinions and more.

title: Ecological diatribes
text: Ecology is a indeed a science. However, a interdisciplinary science! To start, lies in the intersection between math and biology.

title: Your title here
text:
Citations
Add your references by specifying the footer like this:
::: footer
r fa("book", fill = "steelblue") [Sanger et al. 1977 _PNAS_](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.74.12.5463)
:::
Then a link will display the citation in the footer next to the book icon .
In this case a paper from Fred Sanger published in PNAS 1977.

title: Research
text:
My research explores fundamental and modern questions in wildlife ecology and conservation genomics, resonating with Elton’s classic inquiry: What factors determine the distribution and abundance of organisms? and Pianka’s broader question: How do ecological and evolutionary processes shape the diversity of life across landscapes? These guiding questions frame my work on the ecology, adaptation, and conservation of threatened small mammals. By addressing these foundational ecological questions with modern tools such as genomics, ecological niche modelling multivariate statistics and more, my work advances our understanding of biodiversity and provides critical insights for conservation in a rapidly changing world.
I am currently a postdoctoral researcher based at Marine Science Faculty, UABC.
